sourceMovie.title: Antigang
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Antigang for the same reason it broke through in 2015: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Benjamin Rocher keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Tango & Cash
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Antigang clicked for you, Tango & Cash (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Havoc
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Antigang clicked for you, Havoc (2025) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Antigang clicked for you, Death Wish (1974) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Antigang?
faq.answer: Try Tango & Cash, Havoc, Death Wish—each shares DNA with Antigang while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Antigang?
faq.answer: Antigang (2015) is best described as Action, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Antigang part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Antigang - Saga" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
